structuredFacts.title: Interstellar
structuredFacts.subtitle: 2014 film by Christopher Nolan
structuredFacts.leadParagraph:
Interstellar is a 2014 epic science fiction film directed by Christopher Nolan, who co-wrote the screenplay with his brother Jonathan Nolan. It has an ensemble cast led by Matthew McConaughey, Anne Hathaway, Jessica Chastain, Bill Irwin, Ellen Burstyn, and Michael Caine. Set in a dystopian future where Earth is suffering from catastrophic blight and famine, the film follows a group of astronauts who travel through space in search of a new home for humanity.
The film began development in 2006 from a treatment by producer Lynda Obst and theoretical physicist Kip Thorne, and was originally set to be directed by Steven Spielberg. Thorne served as an executive producer and scientific consultant on the film, and wrote the tie-in book The Science of Interstellar. It was Obst's final film as producer before her death. Cinematographer Hoyte van Hoytema shot it on 35 mm film in the Panavision anamorphic format and IMAX 70 mm. Filming began in late 2013 and took place in Alberta, Klaustur, and Los Angeles. Interstellar uses extensive practical and miniature effects, and the company DNEG created additional visual effects.
Interstellar premiered at the TCL Chinese Theatre on October 26, 2014, and was released in theaters in the United States on November 5, and in the United Kingdom on November 7, with Paramount Pictures distributing in the United States and Warner Bros. Pictures distributing in international markets. In the United States, it was first released on film stock, expanding to venues using digital projectors. It was a commercial success, grossing $681 million worldwide during its initial theatrical run, and $774.7 million worldwide with subsequent releases, making it the 10th-highest-grossing film of 2014. The film received generally positive reviews from critics. Among its various accolades, Interstellar was nominated for five awards at the 87th Academy Awards, winning Best Visual Effects.

timeline.headline: == Plot == In , life on Earth is collapsing
timeline.description: == Plot == In 2067, life on Earth is collapsing.

timeline.headline: == Production == === Development and financing === The premise for Interstellar was conceived by the producer Lynda Obst and the theoretical physicist Kip Thorne, who collaborated on the film Contact (), and had known each other since Carl Sagan set them up on a blind date
timeline.description: == Production == === Development and financing === The premise for Interstellar was conceived by the producer Lynda Obst and the theoretical physicist Kip Thorne.

timeline.headline: By March , Jonathan Nolan was hired to write a screenplay
timeline.description: By March 2007, Jonathan Nolan was hired to write a screenplay.

timeline.headline: After Spielberg moved his production studio, DreamWorks Pictures
timeline.description: After Spielberg moved his production studio, DreamWorks Pictures, from Paramount Pictures to Walt Disney Studios in 2009, Paramount needed a new director for Interstellar.

timeline.headline: Jonathan Nolan recommended his brother Christopher
timeline.description: Jonathan Nolan recommended his brother Christopher, who joined the project in 2012.

chapters.title: Interstellar's Story
chapters.readerQuestion: What defines the story of Interstellar?
approvedFacts: Interstellar is a 2014 epic science fiction film directed by Christopher Nolan, who co-wrote the screenplay with his brother Jonathan Nolan. It has an ensemble cast led by Matthew McConaughey, Anne Hathaway, Jessica Chastain, Bill Irwin, Ellen Burstyn, and Michael Caine.

The film began development in 2006 from a treatment by producer Lynda Obst and theoretical physicist Kip Thorne, and was originally set to be directed by Steven Spielberg. Thorne served as an executive producer and scientific consultant on the film, and wrote the tie-in book The Science of Interstellar.

chapters.title: Inside the Production of Interstellar
chapters.readerQuestion: What defines the production of Interstellar?
approvedFacts: Interstellar premiered at the TCL Chinese Theatre on October 26, 2014, and was released in theaters in the United States on November 5, and in the United Kingdom on November 7, with Paramount Pictures distributing in the United States and Warner Bros. Pictures distributing in international markets.

In 2067, life on Earth is collapsing. NASA scientists, Amelia Brand, Romilly, and Doyle, are embarking on an intergalactic mission to find another habitable planet, when former NASA pilot, Cooper, arrives 'by coincidence' to the secret facility.

chapters.title: The Release of Interstellar
chapters.readerQuestion: What defines the release of Interstellar?
approvedFacts: NASA's earlier mission—Lazarus—reported faraway habitable environments, accessible by a wormhole near Saturn. After a two-year voyage, Cooper's crew (including two rectangular shaped robots called TARS and CASE) pass through the wormhole aboard the spacecraft Endurance, emerging into a planetary system orbiting the black hole Gargantua.

On Earth, an older Murph has joined NASA, and works with Brand. She discovers inconsistencies in Brand's equations, which will purportedly guide humanity's mass exodus from Earth once a viable alternative (planet) is discovered.

chapters.title: How the World Received Interstellar
chapters.readerQuestion: What defines the reception of Interstellar?
approvedFacts: When Cooper resolves to return to Earth alone, the planet's previous explorer, Mann, tries to kill him. It emerges that Mann falsified information about his planet's habitability in hopes of being rescued.

Beyond Gargantua's horizon, Cooper and TARS emerge inside a tesseract where time is 'physical'. Cooper sees navigable snapshots of Murph's bedroom-in-time, and succeeds in interacting with objects there.

chapters.title: The Enduring Legacy of Interstellar
chapters.readerQuestion: What defines the legacy of Interstellar?
approvedFacts: Aboard a space-station with a recreated healthy-Earth environment, Cooper reunites with Murph, now older than him. Having assured humanity's continued existence, Murph is nearing death but does not want Cooper to witness it.

Matthew McConaughey as Joseph "Coop" Cooper, a widowed NASA pilot who reluctantly becomes a farmer after the agency is closed by the government, and eventually joins the Endurance mission as the lead pilot.

triviaCandidates.fact: Interstellar is a 2014 epic science fiction film directed by Christopher Nolan, who co-wrote the screenplay with his brother Jonathan Nolan. It has an ensemble cast led by Matthew McConaughey, Anne Hathaway, Jessica Chastain, Bill Irwin, Ellen Burstyn, and Michael Caine.

The film began development in 2006 from a treatment by producer Lynda Obst and theoretical physicist Kip Thorne, and was originally set to be directed by Steven Spielberg. Thorne served as an executive producer and scientific consultant on the film, and wrote the tie-in book The Science of Interstellar.
triviaCandidates.readMoreTopic: Academy Award for Best Visual Effects

triviaCandidates.fact: Interstellar premiered at the TCL Chinese Theatre on October 26, 2014, and was released in theaters in the United States on November 5, and in the United Kingdom on November 7, with Paramount Pictures distributing in the United States and Warner Bros. Pictures distributing in international markets.

In 2067, life on Earth is collapsing. NASA scientists, Amelia Brand, Romilly, and Doyle, are embarking on an intergalactic mission to find another habitable planet, when former NASA pilot, Cooper, arrives 'by coincidence' to the secret facility.

NASA's earlier mission—Lazarus—reported faraway habitable environments, accessible by a wormhole near Saturn. After a two-year voyage, Cooper's crew (including two rectangular shaped robots called TARS and CASE) pass through the wormhole aboard the spacecraft Endurance, emerging into a planetary system orbiting the black hole Gargantua.

On Earth, an older Murph has joined NASA, and works with Brand. She discovers inconsistencies in Brand's equations, which will purportedly guide humanity's mass exodus from Earth once a viable alternative (planet) is discovered.

When Cooper resolves to return to Earth alone, the planet's previous explorer, Mann, tries to kill him. It emerges that Mann falsified information about his planet's habitability in hopes of being rescued.

Beyond Gargantua's horizon, Cooper and TARS emerge inside a tesseract where time is 'physical'. Cooper sees navigable snapshots of Murph's bedroom-in-time, and succeeds in interacting with objects there.

Aboard a space-station with a recreated healthy-Earth environment, Cooper reunites with Murph, now older than him. Having assured humanity's continued existence, Murph is nearing death but does not want Cooper to witness it.

Matthew McConaughey as Joseph "Coop" Cooper, a widowed NASA pilot who reluctantly becomes a farmer after the agency is closed by the government, and eventually joins the Endurance mission as the lead pilot.
